channel.title: Open Bankruptcy Project -- News & Analysis
channel.description: Data-driven bankruptcy news and analysis from the Open Bankruptcy Project. Empirical research covering 4.9 million federal cases across all 94 judicial districts.

item.title: Open Bankruptcy Project Launches 160+ Free Bankruptcy Resources Nationwide
item.dc:creator: Open Bankruptcy Project
item.description: The Open Bankruptcy Project has launched a network of more than 160 free educational websites covering every major bankruptcy topic, with interactive tools, bilingual content in English and Spanish, and data from 4.9 million federal cases. No ads, no attorney referrals, no paywalls.

item.title: 75,764 Estimated Section 1328(f) Violations: No Court Is Screening for Them
item.dc:creator: Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team
item.description: An estimated 75,764 Section 1328(f) timing violations have occurred nationally, and no federal court is systematically screening for them. District-by-district data from 4.9 million cases.

item.title: Where Chapter 13 Fails: The 15 Worst Districts for Plan Completion (2020-2024)
item.dc:creator: Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team
item.description: National analysis of Chapter 13 plan completion rates reveals the 15 worst-performing federal districts from 2020-2024, with dismissal rates exceeding 60% in some courts.

item.title: 1 in 3 Chapter 13 Filers Has Filed Before: Repeat Bankruptcy by District
item.dc:creator: Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team
item.description: District-by-district data on repeat bankruptcy filings shows approximately one-third of Chapter 13 filers have filed previously, raising questions about system effectiveness.

item.title: Attorney Fee Transparency in Consumer Bankruptcy: A National Analysis
item.dc:creator: Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team
item.description: Fee disclosures in consumer bankruptcy happen after retention, lack standardization, and sit behind PACER's paywall. Open Bankruptcy Project data reveals significant concentration in the consumer bankruptcy bar.

item.title: Bankruptcy Filings by State: 2026 National Overview
item.dc:creator: Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team
item.description: Comprehensive state-by-state breakdown of bankruptcy filing rates for 2026, with data on Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 distribution across all 50 states.

item.title: Chapter 13 Completion Rates Vary Widely Across Federal Districts
item.dc:creator: Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team
item.description: National analysis shows Chapter 13 plan completion rates vary dramatically by federal district, ranging from under 20% to over 60%, driven by local legal culture and fee structures.

item.title: The Means Test Gets Harder: April 2026 Median Income Increases
item.dc:creator: Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team
item.description: Updated median income figures for April 2026 raise the bar for the Chapter 7 means test, potentially pushing more filers toward Chapter 13.

item.title: Pro Se Bankruptcy Filings: What the Data Shows
item.dc:creator: Open Bankruptcy Project Research Team
item.description: Data analysis of pro se (self-represented) bankruptcy filings across federal districts reveals patterns in filing rates, outcomes, and the gap between represented and unrepresented debtors.
